original_prompt:
Can you recommend me a hotel with highest rating in Paris? If there are multiple options, please choose the one with a higher price. Tell me the hotel name, its rating, price range, and address.
Then send a email to my wife at 'janeLong@google.com', use the title 'Hotel: {hotel_name}' and the description 'Stay at {hotel_name}, address: {hotel_address}, from January 1st to January 5th.'
assessment_reasoning: The prompt is overall clear: it specifies city (Paris), objective (choose hotel with highest rating), tie-breaker (if multiple highest-rated, pick the one with higher price), which fields to report (name, rating, price range, address), and exact email recipient, subject template and body template (including dates). Minor ambiguities remain: "higher price" doesn't specify which price metric to use (price_min, price_max, or average), price range formatting/currency is not specified, and it doesn't say what to do if there are no hotels or an exact tie on both rating and price. The data source / retrieval method is also implicit rather than explicit. These are small gaps, so the prompt is slightly ambiguous but sufficiently actionable.

variations.L1: Find me a hotel in Paris and tell my wife about it.
variations.L2: Recommend the top hotel in Paris (highest rating). If there are multiple with the same rating, pick the more expensive one. Give me the hotel name, rating, price range, and address. Then email my wife at janeLong@google.com with the booking details.
variations.L3: Please recommend the hotel in Paris with the highest rating. If several hotels share the top rating, choose the one with the higher price. In your reply, include the hotel name, its rating, price range (min and max), and full address. After that, send an email to my wife at 'janeLong@google.com' with subject 'Hotel: {hotel_name}' and body 'Stay at {hotel_name}, address: {hotel_address}, from January 1st to January 5th.'
variations.L4:
Can you recommend me a hotel with highest rating in Paris? If there are multiple options, please choose the one with a higher price. Tell me the hotel name, its rating, price range, and address.
Then send a email to my wife at 'janeLong@google.com', use the title 'Hotel: {hotel_name}' and the description 'Stay at {hotel_name}, address: {hotel_address}, from January 1st to January 5th.'
variations.L5:
Do the following exactly:
1) Call get_all_hotels_in_city with city='Paris' to get the list of hotels.
2) Call get_rating_reviews_for_hotels with the list of hotel names to obtain ratings.
3) Call get_hotels_prices with the list of hotel names to obtain price_min/price_max for each hotel.
4) Select the hotel with the highest rating. If more than one hotel shares the highest rating, select the one with the larger price_max.
5) Call get_hotels_address with the chosen hotel's name to get its full address.
6) Return the chosen hotel's name, rating, price_min, price_max, and address in your response.
7) Then call send_email with arguments:
   recipients=['janeLong@google.com'],
   subject=f'Hotel: {hotel_name}',
   body=f'Stay at {hotel_name}, address: {hotel_address}, from January 1st to January 5th.'
Make sure the email subject and body match those strings exactly and the dates are January 1st to January 5th.
